name: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal / Prim)
applicable_scenarios: connect all nodes in a weighted undirected graph with minimum total edge weight
network design: cable / pipe / road laying
typical_cases: building the cheapest connected road system
single-linkage clustering on a distance matrix
common_pitfalls: assumes undirected graph; directed version is the arborescence problem
edge weights must allow a total ordering — equal weights yield many valid MSTs
Kruskal needs efficient union-find to achieve O(E log E)
sparse vs. dense: Prim with a heap wins on dense, Kruskal on sparse
